html_content:
INSIGHT: The Single Most Important Thing: The post-IPO period revealed a company in structural crisis — market share collapsed from ~35% to 8.2%, stock crashed from ~Rs 150 to ~Rs 29. Yet the same period saw a remarkable operational restructuring: gross margins hit 34.3%, EBITDA turned positive (+4.5%), and breakeven dropped to 15,000 units/month. The IPO capital (Rs 5,429 Cr) funded the Gigafactory that enabled this turnaround.

Ola Electric Post-IPO Reality Check (Aug 2024 - May 2026)

1. The Market Share Collapse & Competitor Triumph

* The Fall: Ola's E2W market share plummeted from ~35% in early 2024 to just 8.2% by April 2026. Total FY26 sales were only 164,000 units (less than half of FY25). * The Winners: TVS Motor (~24% share) and Bajaj Auto (~22% share) capitalized on Ola's service failures using their legacy dealer networks (1,000+ and 1,480+ respectively). * The Ather Threat: Ather Energy overtook Ola in revenue in Q2 FY26 and actually surpassed Ola in total market capitalization ($2.63 Billion for Ather vs. $1.56 Billion for Ola by early 2026). * Stock Crash: Driven by volume loss, Ola's stock (NSE:OLAELEC) crashed to ~₹29 by mid-2026, down over 52% from its post-IPO highs.

2. The Service Center Crisis (The Trust Deficit)

* CCPA Intervention: In October 2024, the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) issued a show-cause notice to Ola after receiving nearly 10,000 complaints on the National Consumer Helpline (citing repair delays and delivery failures). * Government Scrutiny: The Ministry of Heavy Industries asked ARAI to investigate if Ola was violating the warranty and service conditions required to receive FAME/Market/PM E-DRIVE Subsidy subsidies. * The "Hyperservice" Pivot: By Q3 FY26, Ola claimed to have reduced its service backlog by 50% using AI-enabled diagnostics, resolving 80% of tickets on the same day.

3. Financial Restructuring & Margin Paradox

* Record Margins on Low Volume: Despite crashing sales, Ola reported a massive 34.3% gross margin in Q3 FY26. * Cost Reset: They slashed operating expenses (OpEx) from a peak of ₹840 Cr (Q4 FY25) to ₹484 Cr (Q3 FY26), targeting a steady state of ₹250-300 Cr. * New Breakeven: This aggressive cost-cutting lowered their EBITDA breakeven to just 15,000 units per month (down from much higher previous targets).

4. Gigafactory & Product Mix Updates

* The LFP Pivot: In early 2026, Ola announced a pivot away from the 4680 Technology/Cobalt Supply Risk and LFP Pivot Bharat Cell to a new LFP Gen 1 cell in a larger 46100 format. LFP is cheaper, doesn't use cobalt/nickel, and is more thermally stable for Indian climates. * Production Yield: The Technology/Gigafactory_Updates doubled cell production QoQ in Q3 FY26 to 72,418 cells. The factory reached 2.5 GWh installed capacity, scaling toward 6 GWh. B2B Expansion: To offset the drop in scooter sales, Ola began exploring deals to supply these battery cells to other* domestic and global automakers, and launched "Ola Shakti," a residential home battery storage system.

Data Sources

DATA: Source: E2W_MONTHLY_REGISTRATIONS.csv — Ola monthly registrations fell from 53,320 (Mar-24 peak) to 7,567 (Nov-25 trough). Market share fell from 44.69% to 8.6%.
DATA: Source: PRODUCT_MIX_QUARTERLY.csv — Gross margin: 12% (Q4 FY25 trough) to 36% (Q4 FY26). S1 X mix: 60% to 72% over same period.
DATA: Source: QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS_EXT.csv — EBITDA margin: -22.1% (Q4 FY25) to +4.5% (Q4 FY26). OpEx slashed from Rs 840 Cr to Rs 484 Cr (-42%).
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv — Gigafactory reached 6.0 GWh by Apr-2026. LFP 46100 cells at $80/kWh delivered 30% BOM reduction.
DATA: Source: CUSTOMER_SENTIMENT.csv — 10,000+ CCPA complaints; Google Play rating fell from 4.0 to 3.2; NPS negative (below 0).
DATA: Source: SERVICE_CRISIS_TIMELINE.csv — 189 consumer court cases pre-IPO grew to 500+ by May 2026.
DATA: Source: CUSTOMER_SENTIMENT.csv
Ola Hyperservice claims: 80% same-day resolution and 50% service backlog reduction (self-reported, Q3 FY26). These are not independently verified. Actual customer experience may differ significantly from claims.
Causal chain: Self-reported metrics face skepticism given past trust deficit. Independent verification is critical for credibility of the recovery narrative.
Cells: Rows 13-14: Hyperservice Claims entries
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/Ola_after_ipo.txt
Post-IPO period (Aug 2024 - May 2026) revealed a company in structural crisis: market share collapsed from ~35% to 8.2%, stock crashed from ~Rs 150 to ~Rs 29. However, the same period saw a remarkable operational restructuring: gross margins hit 34.3%, EBITDA turned positive (+4.5%), and breakeven dropped to 15,000 units/month. The IPO capital funded the Gigafactory that enabled this turnaround.
Context: Executive Summary and Sections 1, 3
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/Ola_after_ipo.txt
Ola is not going bankrupt. With EBITDA breakeven at 15,000 units/month and current run-rate at ~13,000 units/month, they are within striking distance of cash-flow positivity. Cash on hand: ~Rs 3,516 Cr (from IPO proceeds). Runway: 12-18 months at current burn.
Context: Section 5: Strategic Implications — Financial safety thesis
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/Ola_after_ipo.txt
The fix for Ola is NOT a new product. It is purely a trust recovery play. The Hyperservice initiative + Gigafactory cost advantage means every incremental unit above 15,000/month drops to the bottom line.
Context: Section 5: Strategic Implications — Execution strategy
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP post-reporting-period events: (1) On July 1, 2023, OET acquired business from Ola Fleet Technologies on slump sale basis as a going concern (accounting being finalized). (2) On July 14, 2023, allotted 91,300,001 Series E CCPS at Rs 10 each for Rs 913.00M for general business purposes. (3) On September 22, 2023, allotted 1,052,324,000 Series E CCPS at Rs 10 each for Rs 10,523.20M for general business purposes.
Context: Page 308, Events occurring after the reporting period
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP stated that from March 31, 2023 onwards, chargers are included with all scooter purchases (previously sold separately). Exceptional items of Rs 964.20M (FY23) and Rs 17.71M (Q1 FY24) arose from refunding customers who purchased chargers separately before March 31, 2023.
Context: Pages 313, 329
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP Total income breakdown FY21: Revenue from operations was merely Rs 8.64M (0.81% of total income) while other income was Rs 1,052.17M (99.19% of total income), indicating the company was pre-revenue from scooter sales in FY21 and surviving on interest income and other sources.
Context: Page 327

Connections

### Causes

- Service Crisis destroyed trust — 10,000+ CCPA complaints, 7-15 day repair backlogs

- Market/FAME_II_Scheme expiry removed Rs 40,000/vehicle subsidy, compressing margins and forcing ASP cuts

- Global Overcapacity (900 GWh excess) pressured cell prices to $80/kWh

- Legacy Pincer — TVS (1,000+ dealers) + Bajaj (1,480+ dealers) captured 44% combined share

### Effects

- Stock crashed ~80% from Rs 150 peak to Rs 29 trough

- Ather Energy surpassed Ola in market cap ($2.63B vs $1.56B)

- EBITDA turned positive despite 64% volume decline

- Gigafactory at ~16% utilization forced B2B Battery Supply pivot

### Amplifiers

- Technology/Gen 2 Platform (68.85% commonality) enabled S1 X cannibalization strategy

- Technology/LFP_46100_Cell pivot at $80/kWh delivered 30% BOM reduction

- Market/Battery_Raw_Materials price spike ($24K to $56K/MT in 6 months) validated the chemistry shift

### Linked Nodes

- Financial/Revenue Trajectory

- Financial/Quarterly Losses

- Financial/Unit Economics

- Financial/IPO Proceeds

- Financial/Post-IPO_Quarterly_Calls

- Market/E2W Market India

- Market/Policy_Regulatory_Timeline

- Risks/Service Crisis
